projects.title: Nova Financial
projects.subtitle: A precision-engineered data visualization platform built for high-net-worth individuals who need clarity inside complexity.
projects.desc: Data visualization platform designed for high-net-worth individual management with surgical UI precision.
overview: Nova Financial is a wealth management dashboard engineered for institutional clients who need real-time portfolio analytics without the noise. The core challenge was translating deeply complex financial data into something instantly readable, actionable, and visually authoritative.

Every interaction was designed with intent — from the animated sparklines that respond to hover, to the drill-down charts that reveal deeper data layers without losing spatial context. The result is a platform that respects both the intelligence of its users and the weight of the decisions they make.

challenges.challenge: Rendering thousands of live data points simultaneously without degrading UI performance.
challenges.solution: Adopted a hybrid rendering strategy — D3 canvas for high-density chart areas, SVG reserved exclusively for interactive overlays. This kept frame rates smooth even under heavy data loads.

challenges.challenge: Making dense financial data immediately legible without sacrificing depth.
challenges.solution: Built a three-tier visual hierarchy: primary KPIs at full opacity, secondary metrics at 50%, tertiary data surfaced on demand. Users could scan the dashboard in under three seconds.

projects.title: Glamorix
projects.subtitle: A full-featured beauty e-commerce platform with seamless cart management, secure payments, and a dark/light UI that feels as refined as the products it sells.
projects.desc: A modern, responsive beauty and skincare e-commerce store — featuring authentication, cart, wishlist, PayPal checkout, and a dual-theme UI.
overview: Glamorix is a modern e-commerce platform built for beauty and skincare products — designed to feel as considered and elegant as the brands it showcases. The project covers the full customer journey: product discovery, wishlist curation, cart management, and a smooth PayPal-powered checkout.

As the frontend lead on a collaborative team, I was responsible for architecting the UI, building the component system, implementing the authentication flow, and integrating the PayPal SDK. The app supports both dark and light themes, with a responsive layout that holds up across every screen size.

challenges.challenge: Keeping cart and wishlist state consistent across pages and user sessions without a backend.
challenges.solution: Managed all client state through React context combined with localStorage persistence, ensuring continuity across refreshes and navigation without a server dependency.

challenges.challenge: Building a believable checkout experience with PayPal integration inside a mock-API environment.
challenges.solution: Wired the PayPal React SDK to a JSON Server backend, simulating real order creation and confirmation flows — giving the checkout a production-grade feel in a fully local environment.

challenges.challenge: Delivering a dual-theme UI that felt intentional rather than just toggled colors.
challenges.solution: Designed the theme system from the ground up using DaisyUI's theming layer over Tailwind, defining separate token sets for each mode — the light theme felt warm and airy, the dark theme rich and editorial.
